message:
test(fuzz): add cbor float-canonicalization rules target (resolves o41gx)

Adds fuzz_cbor_float_canonical covering the three rules
canonicalize_value_in_place enforces on Value::Float (lib.rs:492-499):

  (a) NaN  → SerializationError::NonFiniteFloat
  (b) ±Inf → NonFiniteFloat
  (c) -0.0 → normalized to +0.0 in encoded bytes

Existing fuzz_canonicalize_map_deterministic substitutes 0.0 for NaN
inputs (the arbitrary_leaf branch), so the rejection path for
non-finite floats is never exercised by existing fuzz coverage. A
regression dropping that rejection would let an attacker smuggle
distinct NaN bit patterns through the canonical-CBOR boundary —
breaking content-addressing determinism, since every NaN payload
encodes as a different 8-byte sequence.

Properties asserted:

  1. to_canonical_cbor::<f64> is panic-free over every 64-bit pattern
     (NaN, ±Inf, subnormal, finite).
  2. NaN bit patterns (sign × signaling × payload) yield NonFiniteFloat.
  3. ±Infinity yields NonFiniteFloat.
  4. encode(-0.0) byte-equals encode(0.0) — the public-facing
     content-addressing property.
  5. Finite floats round-trip via encode → ciborium decode → encode
     to byte-identical output.

Once-gated regression anchor verifies the negative-zero normalization
plus NaN/+Inf rejection on every fuzz session start, with f64
bit-pattern sanity checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0bo","title":"fcp.docusign: Track Signature Status","description":"# fcp.docusign: Track Signature Status (FCP2)

## Goal
Allow agents to track envelope progress:
- delivered/signed/completed/declined/voided
- recipient-level status (without leaking PII)

## Scope
- Get envelope status.
- List envelopes with filters (date range, status).
- Optional: ingest Connect webhooks for real-time status updates.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `docusign.envelopes.read`
- `docusign.webhook.ingest` (optional)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- PII hygiene:
  - recipient names/emails are sensitive
  - logs include only envelope_id + status + timestamps

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `docusign.envelope.status.get`
  - `docusign.envelope.list`
  - Optional: `docusign.webhook.ingest`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - status parsing
  - webhook replay/idempotency (if enabled)

## Acceptance criteria
- Envelope status tracking is reliable and safe for automation.

No additional work needed.","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:00:23Z"},{"id":1381,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0bo","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented tracking.rs: EnvelopeStatusInfo with progress tracking, RecipientStatus (privacy-safe: no email/name), WebhookEventType (8 types), EnvelopeListQuery builder. 149 tests, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:12:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0cncv","title":"[HARDENING][MEDIUM] fcp-bootstrap cert validity lacks clock-skew tolerance","description":"crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/hardware_token.rs:1223 and :1249 both call parsed.validity().is_valid() to check X.509 NotBefore/NotAfter against system time, with no skew tolerance. A machine with clock drift (cold-boot before NTP sync, VM clock, etc.) can silently reject freshly-provisioned certificates whose NotBefore is seconds in the future from the host's perspective, producing a cryptic NoVerifiedIssuerChain error with no hint that it's a clock issue.

Standard PKI practice is to accept certs within a small skew window (±60s typical).

Fix: add a helper cert_valid_with_skew(&parsed, 60_secs) that uses parsed.validity().not_before / .not_after and OffsetDateTime::now_utc() directly, accepting a forward-skew on NotBefore. Keep NotAfter strict (never accept expired). Apply at both call sites.

Not a vulnerability — defensive hardening for cold-bootstrap robustness on clock-skewed machines.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T03:58:52.658144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T04:27:38.370550Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T04:27:38.370246Z","close_reason":"Completed: added 60s NotBefore bootstrap skew tolerance while keeping NotAfter strict, with certificate-selection regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0iek1","title":"[fcp-e2e] Enable kill_on_drop for connector subprocess runner","description":"crates/fcp-e2e/src/subprocess.rs claimed to avoid zombie leaks in Drop, but it only called Child::start_kill(). The underlying asupersync Child docs state start_kill/kill do not wait and still require wait() to reap. Switch the spawned command to kill_on_drop(true), remove the misleading manual Drop path, and add regression coverage that a dropped long-running runner no longer leaves its PID present.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:44:41.568206Z","created_by":"ChartreuseCardinal","updated_at":"2026-03-25T19:32:33.422700Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T19:32:33.422177Z","close_reason":"Stale-complete: kill_on_drop implementation and regression coverage already present in worktree","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1993,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0iek1","author":"VioletSummit","text":"Verified current worktree state for this bead.

Findings:
- `crates/fcp-e2e/src/subprocess.rs` already sets `Command::kill_on_drop(true)` in `ConnectorProcessRunner::spawn()`.
- The old manual-Drop approach is no longer present.
- A Unix regression test exists at `drop_reaps_long_running_process`, which checks that dropping a long-running runner makes its PID disappear.

Verification:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-e2e --all-targets` passed.
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt -p fcp-e2e -- --check` passed.
- `ubs crates/fcp-e2e/src/subprocess.rs` is not trustworthy for this single Rust file in this repo state; UBS misclassified the path across multiple language scanners and emitted obvious false positives like JavaScript loose-equality warnings on Rust syntax.
- A targeted `rch exec -- cargo test -p fcp-e2e --lib drop_reaps_long_running_process -- --nocapture` did not reach the `fcp-e2e` test body because the remote build hit an unrelated compile blocker in `crates/fcp-conformance/src/vectors/hpke.rs` on the worker, despite the local worktree already containing a fix for that line.
- Earlier remote test runs also hit separate `rch` artifact-retrieval failures caused by pathological nested paths under `.beads/recovery_*`; that is operational fallout, not evidence against this bead implementation.

Conclusion: the requested `kill_on_drop` change and regression coverage are already present in the current worktree, so this bead is stale-complete from a backlog perspective. Remaining noise is in unrelated verification infrastructure.","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:32:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","title":"[FCP3/P4.3] Unify runtime state, error mapping, and health and checkpoint adapters","description":"Background: even with shared kernel and SDK contracts, runtime state and failure handling can drift if error mappings, health semantics, and checkpoint adapters are implemented separately by host, SDK, and connector families.

Scope: unify runtime state vocabulary, error mapping rules, health and reporting semantics, and checkpoint adapters across archetypes so request response, streaming, webhook, and similar patterns do not each invent incompatible interpretations. Standardize the structured lifecycle and health and checkpoint log fields emitted at each transition so later integration and end to end harnesses can correlate host, SDK, and connector behavior without manual interpretation.

Acceptance: runtime behavior is explainable in one vocabulary across host, SDK, and connector implementations, and the same vocabulary powers diagnostics and regression tests instead of spawning parallel interpretations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:06.439780Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:30:42.148877Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T18:13:50.136219Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","health","mesh-first","phase-4","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1fu5d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:33.589697Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:33.073860Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2213,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-01: Claiming after closing blocker tbjdk (P4.1). Starting with code archaeology: mapping runtime state vocabulary across fcp-host, fcp-sdk, fcp-kernel, and connector archetypes to identify divergent error mappings, health semantics, and checkpoint adapter patterns. Goal: one vocabulary for runtime behavior that powers diagnostics and tests across all execution forms.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:46:52Z"},{"id":2217,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"Claiming a narrow 0jm9a follow-on in crates/fcp-host/src/health.rs only. Current host diagnostics still carry connector runtime status and connector identity as raw strings inside ConnectorProcessHealth even though the host already has a concrete ProcessState model and kernel-owned ConnectorId type. Planned change: replace the stringly-typed connector/process fields with typed host-local/runtime vocabulary while preserving the serialized health JSON shape.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:53:05Z"},{"id":2219,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"Completed a narrow health.rs follow-on. ConnectorProcessHealth now uses fcp_kernel::ConnectorId and a typed ConnectorProcessState enum instead of raw String identity/state fields, and the host-local process-state enum serializes with the same snake_case JSON labels as before. I also added a ProcessState -> ConnectorProcessState adapter and updated the local health tests to use canonical connector IDs so this diagnostics surface no longer accepts arbitrary string drift. Local rustfmt --edition 2024 and git diff --check passed on crates/fcp-host/src/health.rs. Remote verification used RCH_VERBOSE=1 rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-host --tests from crates/fcp-host; exact sync completed successfully, remote dependency preflight verified 26 roots on worker vmi1149989, remote cargo then failed because nightly-2026-02-18-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu is not installed on that worker, and I interrupted the local fail-open fallback immediately.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:58:04Z"},{"id":2222,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-01: Added canonical HTTP→FCP error mapping in fcp-sdk/src/migration.rs: map_http_status(status, service, message) provides single source of truth for HTTP-to-FCP error taxonomy. Maps 400→InvalidRequest(1001), 401→Unauthorized(2001), 403→CapabilityDenied, 404→ResourceNotFound, 408→UpstreamTimeout, 429→RateLimited, 5xx→External(retryable for 500/502/503/504). Also added is_http_status_retryable(status) for consistent retry decisions. 11 new tests, 955 SDK tests pass via rch. Connectors should adopt map_http_status instead of ad-hoc mappings. Next: address Slack ConnectorErrorMapping gap and health vocabulary consolidation.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:12:51Z"},{"id":2223,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-01: Acceptance verification for 0jm9a.

RUNTIME STATE VOCABULARY: Unified via fcp-kernel (LifecycleState, HealthState, ConnectorHealth, SelfCheckReport). Host consumes kernel types through re-exports. No parallel public definitions.

ERROR MAPPING: (a) Canonical map_http_status(status, service, message) in fcp-sdk provides single source of truth for HTTP→FCP error taxonomy with 11 tests. (b) ConnectorErrorMapping trait is implemented across all reviewed connectors (Slack, Discord, GitHub, Telegram, Monday). (c) Error codes standardized: 401→code 2001, 403→CapabilityDenied, 404→ResourceNotFound, 429→RateLimited, 5xx→External.

HEALTH REPORTING: HealthSnapshot, SelfCheckReport, ConnectorHealth, RateLimitStatus all standardized in fcp-core. Host's parallel HealthState is private (dead code, not exported).

CHECKPOINT ADAPTERS: ComputationCheckpoint, ConnectorStateSnapshot, ConnectorStateModel, CursorState all unified in fcp-core with canonical CBOR encoding and schema versioning.

STRUCTURED LOGGING: Rollout/cancellation/progress use kernel vocabulary for tracing fields. SDK and host emit connector_id, operation, state transitions. Full log schema contract deferred to a dedicated bead.

955 fcp-sdk tests pass. Acceptance criteria met: runtime behavior is explainable in one vocabulary across host, SDK, and connector implementations.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:13:43Z"},{"id":2226,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"Claiming a narrow doctor.rs follow-on under 0jm9a. Scope: keep DoctorRequest raw so HTTP input validation behavior stays stable, but move DoctorReport.zone_id plus ConnectorSelfCheck.connector_id and derived CheckResult.connector_id onto canonical ZoneId / ConnectorId types. The serialized JSON shape should remain string-valued for these fields.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:20:57Z"},{"id":2231,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"WildTern","text":"Completed doctor typed-identifier follow-up. Kept DoctorRequest.zone_id/connectors raw for request validation stability, but finished migrating DoctorReport.zone_id, ConnectorSelfCheck.connector_id, and CheckResult.connector_id to typed ZoneId/ConnectorId coverage across crates/fcp-host/src/doctor.rs and crates/fcp-host/tests/no_mock_integration.rs. Normalized invalid legacy fixtures to canonical IDs/zones, added fixture ConnectorId helpers, and updated typed assertions/serde round-trips. Verification: rustfmt --edition 2024 passed; git diff --check passed; RCH_VERBOSE=1 rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-host --tests from crates/fcp-host completed 26-root remote dependency preflight on worker vmi1149989, reached remote Cargo, then failed because the worker is missing nightly-2026-02-15-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu. Interrupted the local fail-open fallback immediately when it started waiting on the package-cache lock.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:30:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0k3d4","title":"[REVIEW-POSTMORTEM][P2] jdaro legacy track() shim leaves untracked-owner ops cross-cancellable","description":"Post-session fresh-eyes review of commit 8c49b234 (br-jdaro). The fix correctly protects operations WITH X-Principal headers: invoke_handler now calls track_with_owner(&operation_id, asserted_principal.as_deref()), and cancel() enforces principal match before any state mutation.

Residual gap: the legacy track() shim (cancellation.rs:~90) still exists as a deliberate backward-compat path for unauthenticated integration tests: 'None preserves legacy untracked-owner semantics (any caller may cancel)'. The regression test cancel_allows_any_principal_when_no_owner_recorded pins this behavior.

Per AGENTS.md: 'We do not care about backwards compatibility—we're in early development with no users. We want to do things the RIGHT way with NO TECH DEBT. Never create compatibility shims. Never create wrapper functions for deprecated APIs. Just fix the code directly.' The track() → track_with_owner(id, None) shim is exactly such a compat shim.

Impact is low right now because no production caller outside invoke_handler touches these APIs (grep confirmed). But if a future caller forgets to pass a principal, operations silently become cross-cancellable without any compile-time warning.

Suggested fix: (1) delete the track() shim; (2) migrate the ~60 inline unit tests in cancellation.rs to call track_with_owner(id, None) explicitly (mechanical find-and-replace in the same module); (3) delete the companion regression test cancel_allows_any_principal_when_no_owner_recorded OR reframe it as 'unauthenticated path rejects at cancel' to tighten semantics further; (4) consider whether invoke_handler should require X-Principal entirely (reject missing header) rather than silently tracking with None.

Scope: small (~60 test-call-site mechanical update + single prod-API deletion + ~1-2 test adjustments). Improves compile-time safety + aligns with AGENTS.md anti-shim policy.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T21:23:58.112902Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T00:20:35.928699Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T00:20:35.928330Z","close_reason":"Removed legacy CancellationController::track shim and migrated tests to explicit track_with_owner(..., None).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-nw3xn","title":"[FCP3/P4] Converge host and SDK on one execution model","description":"Background: once kernel, policy, evidence, and durable semantics exist as real owners, fcp-host and fcp-sdk must converge on one execution model. The host should orchestrate and supervise; the SDK should express the same lifecycle and semantics for connector authors; neither should redefine the platform.

Scope: refactor host into a consumer of those lower layers, publish a neutral SDK app contract, and adapt at least one connector family end to end. The phase must cover lifecycle, checkpoint, drain, health, resume, and error semantics with crate local unit tests, cross crate integration coverage, connector author ergonomics checks, and scripted end to end transcripts that include detailed structured diagnostics for both operator and connector author workflows. This phase is complete when host and SDK describe the same runtime, checkpoint, drain, health, and error semantics rather than parallel interpretations.

Acceptance: connector authors and host operators can rely on one execution vocabulary, one set of diagnostics, and reproducible test and transcript artifacts that prove the shared model in practice rather than only in architecture prose.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:01.782511Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-01T19:02:53.482981Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T19:02:53.482824Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","host","mesh-first","phase-4","sdk"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-nw3xn","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:13.291708Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-nw3xn","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1fu5d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:13.091610Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-nw3xn","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-6t9s0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:13.495939Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-nw3xn","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ciy20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:13.706332Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-nw3xn","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:12.898042Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2051,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-nw3xn","author":"CobaltHarbor","text":"Host convergence is the point where the platform stops being conceptually centered on fcp-host. The host remains important, but as a node local root app over shared semantics rather than the place where platform truth is invented.","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-o1na6","title":"[PERF] fcp-bootstrap::open_authenticated_session: global PKCS#11 lock serializes all sessions","description":"crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/hardware_token.rs:451-503 and 585-597. open_authenticated_session() grabs provider_probe_lock() before PKCS#11 initialize/open/login and keeps that MutexGuard alive inside the session close closure until the caller eventually closes the authenticated session. Enumeration sessions do the same for their whole lifetime. That turns hardware-token rotation, cert enumeration, and key enumeration into a process-wide serialized critical section: one long-lived session blocks unrelated providers/slots from even starting, so throughput collapses under parallel bootstrap or rotation work. Proposed improvement: narrow the lock to the init/finalize transition or make it provider-scoped/refcounted instead of holding one global mutex for the full lifetime of every authenticated or enumeration session.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:56:28.975339Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:46:18.096552Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:46:18.096214Z","close_reason":"PKCS#11 session opens now use provider-scoped active-session accounting so init/finalize stay serialized without holding the global lock for the full authenticated session lifetime","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-o8umn","title":"[FCP3/P6.3] Align doctor, preflight, show, health, and explain with durable evidence bundles","description":"Background: commands like doctor, preflight, show, health, and explain should ultimately be grounded in durable evidence bundles and canonical control plane semantics, not in scattered host local heuristics.

Scope: align these operator flows with the new evidence and durable plane model so they can explain not just current availability, but why the system believes a claim, what evidence backs it, which bundle or correlation handle to inspect next, and whether the claim is offline, host local, mesh backed, degraded, or fallback derived. Require operator friendly remediation hints, concise summary paths, deep drill down paths, and detailed structured diagnostic logs so failures can be investigated without code archaeology.

Acceptance: operator diagnostics and explanations use a durable and evidence backed model rather than ad hoc host local state scraping, and they materially improve a user's ability to understand and act on system state.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:08.359361Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-06T04:06:25.759003Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T04:06:25.758786Z","close_reason":"All 3 children closed: o8umn.1 (evidence-lookup model), o8umn.2 (remediation-aware outputs), o8umn.3 (snapshot proof). 78 truth tests cover full operator evidence pipeline.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["evidence","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","logging","mesh-first","operator","phase-6"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-o8umn","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-0qtvu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:50.284483Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-o8umn","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:49.581222Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-o8umn","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:48.991786Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,166 @@
+#![no_main]
+
+//! Fuzz target for fcp-cbor float canonicalization rules.
+//!
+//! `canonicalize_value_in_place` (lib.rs:492-499) enforces three rules
+//! on every `Value::Float` in the canonicalization tree:
+//!
+//!   (a) NaN  → `SerializationError::NonFiniteFloat`
+//!   (b) ±Inf → `SerializationError::NonFiniteFloat`
+//!   (c) -0.0 → normalized to +0.0 before encoding
+//!
+//! The existing `fuzz_canonicalize_map_deterministic` target explicitly
+//! substitutes `0.0` for NaN inputs (the `arbitrary_leaf` branch), so
+//! the rejection path for non-finite floats is **never exercised** by
+//! existing fuzz coverage. A regression that dropped the rejection
+//! (e.g., a refactor that moved the check past the encode boundary)
+//! would let an attacker smuggle distinct NaN bit patterns through the
+//! canonical-CBOR boundary, breaking content-addressing determinism
+//! (every NaN payload encodes as a *different* 8-byte sequence under
+//! ciborium's IEEE-754 round-trip).
+//!
+//! Properties asserted:
+//!
+//!   1. `to_canonical_cbor::<f64>` is panic-free over every 64-bit bit
+//!      pattern (whether it represents NaN, ±Inf, subnormal, or finite).
+//!   2. NaN bit patterns (sign × signaling × payload) MUST yield
+//!      `SerializationError::NonFiniteFloat`.
+//!   3. ±Infinity MUST yield `NonFiniteFloat`.
+//!   4. **Negative zero canonicalization**: `to_canonical_cbor(-0.0)`
+//!      MUST byte-equal `to_canonical_cbor(0.0)` — the public-facing
+//!      property that downstream content-addressing relies on.
+//!   5. Every accepted (finite) float round-trips through
+//!      `ciborium::from_reader::<f64, _>` to a value that re-encodes to
+//!      the same bytes.
+
+use arbitrary::{Arbitrary, Unstructured};
+use ciborium::value::Value;
+use fcp_cbor::{to_canonical_cbor, SerializationError};
+use libfuzzer_sys::fuzz_target;
+use std::sync::Once;
+
+static NEG_ZERO_ANCHOR: Once = Once::new();
+
+#[derive(Arbitrary, Debug)]
+struct Input {
+    /// Reinterpreted as f64 to reach every IEEE-754 bit pattern,
+    /// including NaN payloads ciborium otherwise can't round-trip.
+    bits: u64,
+}
+
+fuzz_target!(|data: &[u8]| {
+    NEG_ZERO_ANCHOR.call_once(assert_neg_zero_canonicalizes);
+
+    let mut u = Unstructured::new(data);
+    let Ok(input) = Input::arbitrary(&mut u) else {
+        return;
+    };
+
+    let f = f64::from_bits(input.bits);
+
+    // ── PROPERTY 1: to_canonical_cbor is total over all f64 patterns ──
+    let result = to_canonical_cbor(&f);
+
+    if f.is_nan() || f.is_infinite() {
+        // ── PROPERTY 2 + 3: NaN / Infinity rejection ──────────────────
+        match result {
+            Err(SerializationError::NonFiniteFloat) => {}
+            Err(other) => panic!(
+                "non-finite f64 (bits=0x{:016x}) MUST trip NonFiniteFloat, \
+                 not {other:?}",
+                input.bits
+            ),
+            Ok(_) => panic!(
+                "non-finite f64 (bits=0x{:016x}, is_nan={}, is_inf={}) was \
+                 accepted by canonicalization",
+                input.bits,
+                f.is_nan(),
+                f.is_infinite()
+            ),
+        }
+        return;
+    }
+
+    // ── PROPERTY 5: finite round-trip ──────────────────────────────────
+    let bytes = result.expect("finite f64 MUST canonicalize without error");
+
+    // Decode and re-encode; byte equality is what downstream content
+    // addressing relies on.
+    let Ok(decoded) = ciborium::from_reader::<Value, _>(&bytes[..]) else {
+        panic!(
+            "canonical bytes for finite f64 (bits=0x{:016x}) failed to \
+             round-trip through ciborium decoder",
+            input.bits
+        );
+    };
+
+    let recanonical =
+        to_canonical_cbor(&decoded).expect("decoded value of canonical bytes must re-encode");
+    assert_eq!(
+        bytes, recanonical,
+        "encode → decode → encode produced different bytes for f64 \
+         (bits=0x{:016x})",
+        input.bits
+    );
+
+    // ── PROPERTY 4 (subset): negative-zero specifically ─────────────────
+    // If the input was -0.0, the canonical bytes must equal +0.0's
+    // canonical bytes — the lib.rs:496-498 normalization rule.
+    if input.bits == (-0.0_f64).to_bits() {
+        let pos_zero_bytes = to_canonical_cbor(&0.0_f64).expect("0.0 canonicalizes");
+        assert_eq!(
+            bytes, pos_zero_bytes,
+            "-0.0 canonical bytes diverged from +0.0 — content-addressing \
+             would assign distinct ObjectIds to logically equal payloads"
+        );
+    }
+});
+
+/// Hand-crafted regression anchor. Run once per process so we always
+/// catch a regression that drops the negative-zero normalization.
+/// Constructed inputs (rather than fuzzer-discovered) because the
+/// fuzzer would only hit the exact 0x8000_0000_0000_0000 pattern by
+/// chance.
+fn assert_neg_zero_canonicalizes() {
+    let neg_zero = -0.0_f64;
+    let pos_zero = 0.0_f64;
+    assert_eq!(
+        neg_zero.to_bits(),
+        0x8000_0000_0000_0000,
+        "f64 sanity: -0.0 bit pattern shifted under us"
+    );
+    assert_ne!(
+        neg_zero.to_bits(),
+        pos_zero.to_bits(),
+        "f64 sanity: -0.0 and 0.0 must differ at the bit level"
+    );
+
+    let nz_bytes = to_canonical_cbor(&neg_zero).expect("-0.0 canonicalizes (lib.rs:496-498)");
+    let pz_bytes = to_canonical_cbor(&pos_zero).expect("0.0 canonicalizes");
+    assert_eq!(
+        nz_bytes, pz_bytes,
+        "negative-zero normalization regression: encode(-0.0) ≠ encode(0.0). \
+         Content-addressed objects whose only difference is the sign bit on \
+         a zero value would now hash to different ObjectIds"
+    );
+
+    // Also anchor that NaN explicitly trips the rejection — guards
+    // against a refactor that drops the is_nan branch.
+    let nan = f64::from_bits(0x7ff8_0000_0000_0001);
+    assert!(
+        matches!(
+            to_canonical_cbor(&nan),
+            Err(SerializationError::NonFiniteFloat)
+        ),
+        "canonical NaN payload must trip NonFiniteFloat (lib.rs:493-494)"
+    );
+
+    // And the +Inf rejection.
+    assert!(
+        matches!(
+            to_canonical_cbor(&f64::INFINITY),
+            Err(SerializationError::NonFiniteFloat)
+        ),
+        "+Infinity must trip NonFiniteFloat"
+    );
+}
